title: Iran Household Expenditure and Income Surveys (HEIS) — documentation, cleaning methodology, and published trend tables (M. Hoseini)
source_org: Mohammad Hoseini (Institute for Management and Planning Studies, Iran), independent researcher documentation site
time_coverage: documentation describes HEIS survey years 1969(1348 SH, rural start)/1963(1342 SH, urban start)-2021(1400 SH); example trend tables/charts in chapters 5-7 span roughly 1996-2021 depending on series
frequency: static reference documentation (not a periodic release)
units: n/a (documentation); embedded tables use rial and share/percent as noted per chapter
license_terms: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike), per site footer
notes: This is a full bookdown-rendered guide (9 HTML chapter pages + index) to SCI's HEIS survey: sample design, rotating panel structure, four-part questionnaire (member characteristics, household facilities, expenditure, income), R code and step-by-step methodology for importing raw MS Access files and cleaning them into item/individual/household-level datasets, PLUS substantive results chapters with embedded data tables and charts: 'Trends in expenditure items' (COICOP group shares, per-capita expenditure by region), 'Trends of household-level data' (9 embedded HTML tables + 2 charts -- verified via regex scan), 'Trends in individual-level data', and 'Some applications' (poverty impact of the November 2019 gas price reform). Satisfies task 2a (codebook/methodology, no registration required) AND substantially task 2b (published aggregate/trend tables derived from IHEIS microdata, not requiring the raw microdata itself) in a single coherent source, so no separate academic-paper appendices were pursued given cost/time budget for this pass. Companion repo github.com/IPRCIRI/IRHEIS (R processing code, not fetched here -- code only, no data/documentation beyond what this site already covers) and github.com/m-hoseini/HEIS (GitHub Pages source repo for this exact site, confirms authorship/currency) checked but not separately downloaded to avoid duplication. Does NOT include the raw IHEIS microdata itself -- see data/raw/iheis-microdata-metadata/access-instructions.md for how to obtain that via SCI registration.
